title: metrosideros microphylla
intro: Metrosideros microphylla, also known as the New Zealand rata or mairehau, is a small to medium-sized tree native to New Zealand. It has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Metrosideros microphylla is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed for their medicinal and therapeutic properties.
